frameworks.name: inverted encoding model
frameworks.one_line: method recovering channel response functions from population brain activity

frameworks.name: linear forward encoding model
frameworks.one_line: linear mapping from assumed channel basis to measured population activity

frameworks.name: stimulus reconstruction
frameworks.one_line: reformulating IEM output to reconstruct the stimulus rather than channel responses

core_question: Do inverted encoding models recover a unique description of population-level stimulus representation?
key_assumptions: population responses are linear combinations of channel basis functions
channel basis functions are chosen a priori by the experimenter
channel response functions are interpreted as population tuning
manipulations: choice of channel basis (unimodal vs bimodal vs random)
reformulating the readout target from channel responses to stimulus reconstruction
key_findings: channel response functions are determined only up to an invertible linear transform
bimodal and random channel bases fit population responses equally well
stimulus reconstruction yields a unimodal function peaked at the true stimulus regardless of channel choice
interpretations: IEM channel response functions are arbitrary and not a unique assay of population tuning
the approach is salvageable when reframed as stimulus reconstruction giving a measure of population selectivity
limitations_noted: channel response function is identifiable only up to invertible linear transform
